title: Amylotheca hollrungii
taxonomy: Amylotheca hollrungii is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to New Guinea. Within the Ericaceae family, Amylotheca hollrungii belongs to the subfamily Epacridoideae. The subfamily Epacridoideae includes several genera, with Amylotheca being one of them. This classification helps to group similar plant species together for further study and identification.
uses: Amylotheca hollrungii is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea. It has been traditionally used by local communities for its medicinal properties, including treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's bark and leaves are used to make teas or infusions that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.
